post.content:
Recently interviewed at Uber and was asked the following question:

**Given a 2D matrix of integers having only 0, 1 and 2.**

**0 repesents empty space**
**1 repesents robot**
**2 repesents blocker**

There can be many robots in the matrix. The task was to return all the robots as per the query below:

**[2, 4, 3, 5]** which means return all robots which have blockers at distance:
2 on the left from the current position of the robot
4 at the top from the current position of the robot
3 on the right from the current position of the robot
5 at the bottom from the current position of the robot

There can be several such queries.

Was able to come up with a simple brute-force solution. But later got a hint from the interviewer that it can be solved using DP.

What could be the DP solution for this? Also, any tips to analyse and come up with a DP solution in an interview will be appreciated.

Does this work?
We traverse the entire grid 4 times. Once each from each direction (row-wise left to right, row-wise right to left, column-wise top to bottom, column-wise bottom to top).
Say we are traversing from left to right. As we traverse, we keep a track of the distance of the current position from the nearest obstacle on the left. If the current cell is an obstacle, we reset this distance to 0. At the end of this traversal, we will have distances of all robots from the closest obstacles on their left.
We do this 4 times, and we have distances of the robots from their closest obstacles in all directions. Something like :
`{(x1,y1):(a1,b1,c1,d1), (x2,y2):(a2,b2,c2,d2), (x3,y3) : (a1,b1,c1,d1)}`
Here (x,y) represents the location of the robot, (a,b,c,d) represents the distances from the closest obstacles in the 4 directions. Then we reverse this map, resulting in something like this:
`{(a1,b1,c1,d1) : [(x1,y1),(x3,y3)], (a2,b2,c2,d2) : [(x2,y2)]}`
This pre-computation takes O(row*columns) time. Its independent of the number of robots in the grid.

At query time, we just lookup this map.

Assumption: We want the robot to visit a place in min possible steps, but if some other robot can visit the same cell in lesser step then it can ignore visiting that cell.
This is a BFS problem using a min heap.

1. Insert all the robot location in Priority Queue
2. For every single iteration take out all the robots and explore all 4 adjacent cells (if Valid -> Not out of matrix and not blocked)
3. Case 1: If the cell is unvisited yet then update the no. of iteration it took to reach the cell
    Case 2: If the cell is visited then ignore.
4. Put the newly visited cell in PQ and continue the loop till no more new cell to explore. 
5. Now for all the queries you can directly return the answer

Time Complexity -> O((Row * Col - Blockers)log (Robots))
Space: O(Robots) 

If we don't want the assumption:
Then we can create a class for robot and it can contain a HashMap<Integer, ArrayList<ArrayList<>> -> Distance, List of [Row, Col]

While doing the BFS update the Robot's map and do a search query in O(1) for R robots. 
Search Time Complexity: O(R)

Since every query can be of size Q:
Total Search Time : O(QR)
Does one robot block another robot?\nLike for Example let\'s say one of the row has values like 10012. For First robot, Is the blocker distance on right is 3(robot) or 4(blocker)?
